fixed_acidity.description: Most acids involved with wine are fixed or nonvolatile (do not evaporate readily).

volatile_acidity.description: The amount of acetic acid in wine, which at too high of levels can lead to an unpleasant vinegar taste.

citric_acid.description: Found in small quantities, citric acid can add freshness and flavor to wines.

residual_sugar.description: The amount of sugar remaining after fermentation stops. Wines with greater than 45 grams/liter are considered sweet.

chlorides.description: The amount of salt in the wine.

free_sulfur_dioxide.description: The free form of SO2 exists in equilibrium between molecular SO2 and bisulfite ion. It prevents microbial growth and wine oxidation.

total_sulfur_dioxide.description: Amount of free and bound forms of SO2; above 50 ppm it becomes evident in the nose and taste of wine.

density.description: The density of the wine, depending on alcohol and sugar content.

pH.description: Describes how acidic or basic a wine is on a scale from 0 (very acidic) to 14 (very basic). Most wines are between 3-4.

sulphates.description: A wine additive which can contribute to sulfur dioxide levels, acting as an antimicrobial and antioxidant.

alcohol.description: The percent alcohol content of the wine.
